EUR.EUR/USD: The euro is seeing gains capped by hawkish Fed rate-hike bets and a loss of momentum in crude oil as prices slip below the 100-hour moving average. While ECB President Christine Lagarde has justified recent tightening and the bank debates doubling minimum reserve requirements to 2%, preliminary June data showing German inflation cooling to 2.4% has eased immediate pressure on the ECB, which remains on hold for the July meeting
EUR.EUR/GBP: EUR/GBP remains range-bound as both the ECB and BoE maintain on-hold stances for their upcoming July meetings. The pair is influenced by a hawkish ECB bias regarding reserve requirements, while BoE Governor Bailey indicates the central bank has sufficient time to assess energy price pass-through, keeping the pound steady
EUR.EUR/JPY: EUR/JPY is supported by the massive carry advantage as JPY COT LF net shorts reach -115.0k with the BoJ on hold. Despite ECB inflation cooling in major economies like Germany, the euro maintains a relative yield edge over the yen, which has hit 39-year lows against the dollar amid historic weakness
EUR.EUR/AUD: The euro faces pressure against the Australian dollar as hawkish RBA minutes and a break of the 100-hour MA in AUD/USD bolster the antipodean currency. EUR LF net short positioning of -67.5k reflects institutional caution even as the ECB maintains a hawkish bias toward one final rate hike
EUR.EUR/CAD: EUR/CAD is influenced by a significant divergence in positioning, with CAD COT LF net shorts at -87.1k and EUR LF net shorts at -67.5k. While Canada's April GDP beat expectations at 0.5%, soft May guidance and the ECB's discussion of higher reserve requirements are keeping the cross in a volatile consolidation pattern
EUR.EUR/CHF: EUR/CHF is supported by an improving Swiss economic outlook as the KOF Barometer climbs above its long-term average, though the SNB remains on hold until September. EUR LF net short positioning of -67.5k remains a headwind for the pair as the ECB balances cooling inflation data against a sustained hawkish policy bias
EUR.EUR/NZD: The euro is underperforming against the New Zealand dollar as a July 8 hike is priced in for the RBNZ, creating a significant OIS disadvantage for the ECB. EUR LF net short positioning of -67.5k further weighs on the pair as the market favors the NZD's leading G10 performance and carry profile

GBP.GBP/USD: The British pound is trading steadily as Governor Bailey notes the BoE has time to judge energy price pass-through, with the central bank expected to stay on hold on July 30. Cable upside is limited as crude oil buying runs out of steam and prices move back below the 100-hour moving average, supporting the dollar's broader resilience
GBP.GBP/JPY: GBP/JPY remains bid as JPY COT LF net shorts hit -115.0k and the BoJ stays on hold, reinforcing the yen's status as the primary G10 laggard. The pound is benefiting from a lack of dovish catalysts, while the yen has plummeted to its weakest level since 1986, keeping intervention risks in focus
GBP.EUR/GBP: The pair is consolidating as EUR LF net short positioning of -67.5k meets a neutral BoE outlook. While ECB's Lagarde maintains a hawkish bias, cooling June inflation in the euro zone has reduced the urgency for immediate hikes, leaving the pound to trade on domestic stability and Prime Minister Starmer's new defense spending plans
GBP.GBP/AUD: GBP/AUD is pressured by the Australian dollar's advance following hawkish RBA minutes and a technical break of the 100-hour MA. With the BoE on hold and no major UK catalysts, the pair is sensitive to the RBA's relative hawkishness and the high volatility premium currently priced into AUD options
GBP.GBP/CAD: GBP/CAD price action is framed by extreme institutional positioning, with CAD COT LF net shorts at -87.1k. Although Canada's April GDP grew by 0.5%, the BoE's steady hand and the BoC's on-hold stance for July 15 keep the pair focused on the divergence between retail sentiment and institutional shorts in the loonie
GBP.GBP/CHF: The pound is steady against the Swiss franc as the SNB remains on hold until late September. While the Swiss KOF Barometer shows an improving economic outlook, the lack of immediate BoE policy shifts and a neutral risk environment (VIX 16.5) keep the pair within recent technical ranges
GBP.GBP/NZD: GBP/NZD is trading with a downward bias as the RBNZ is the only G10 central bank with a hike priced in for its July 8 meeting. The pound lacks the carry support seen in the kiwi, which currently leads G10 performance behind a 62% probability of a rate increase

JPY.USD/JPY: USD/JPY has broken through the 162 level to hit a 39-year low, driven by a 275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage and JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k. While crude oil prices slipping below the 100-hour MA provides a minor headwind for the USD, the pair remains bid as the BoJ stays on hold and the USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation breaks
JPY.EUR/JPY: EUR/JPY is supported by the ECB's hawkish bias and JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k, even as EUR LF net shorts sit at -67.5k. The yen's historic slide to four-decade lows against the dollar is spilling over into euro crosses, as the BoJ remains on hold for its July 31 meeting
JPY.GBP/JPY: The pair is trending higher as the BoE stays on hold and the yen remains under intense pressure, hitting its weakest levels since 1986. JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k reflect a crowded carry trade that continues to punish the yen despite verbal warnings from BOJ policymaker Sato regarding exchange rate volatility
JPY.AUD/JPY: AUD/JPY is surging as the RBA's hawkish minutes contrast with the BoJ's on-hold stance, supported by JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k. The Australian dollar is finding additional support from firmer US confidence data, while the yen fails to track global yield movements, remaining the primary G10 laggard
JPY.CAD/JPY: CAD/JPY is volatile as both currencies face extreme positioning, with CAD COT LF net shorts at -87.1k and JPY at -115.0k. While Canada's April GDP beat expectations, the BoJ's continued hold and the yen's historic weakness are allowing the loonie to maintain its carry advantage despite soft May guidance from Ottawa
JPY.CHF/JPY: CHF/JPY is trading higher as the yen's broad-based decline offsets the SNB's on-hold status. JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k and the BoJ's refusal to shift policy before July 31 keep the yen weak, while the Swiss KOF Barometer indicates a strengthening domestic outlook
JPY.NZD/JPY: NZD/JPY is a primary G10 theme as the RBNZ has a hike priced in for July 8, providing a +335bp OIS carry advantage over the yen. JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k highlight the crowded nature of this carry trade as the yen hits 40-year lows and the BoJ remains on hold

AUD.AUD/USD: The Australian dollar is advancing toward the 0.6913 level, supported by hawkish RBA minutes and a break of the 100-hour MA. While crude oil prices falling below their 100-hour MA weighs on the USD, the AUD/USD volatility premium remains the highest in the G10 at 10.6%, signaling significant tail risk despite the RBA's on-hold stance for August
AUD.EUR/AUD: EUR/AUD is under pressure as EUR LF net shorts of -67.5k meet a resurgent Australian dollar. The RBA's hawkish minutes and firmer US confidence data have bolstered AUD, while the ECB faces cooling inflation in Germany, reducing the relative appeal of the euro
AUD.GBP/AUD: GBP/AUD is trending lower as the Australian dollar benefits from hawkish central bank minutes and a technical breakout. The BoE remains on hold with Governor Bailey signaling a patient approach, leaving the pound vulnerable to the AUD's superior momentum and high options-implied volatility
AUD.AUD/JPY: AUD/JPY is capitalizing on the yen's historic weakness, with JPY COT LF net shorts at -115.0k. The RBA's hawkish tone and the BoJ's on-hold status for July 31 create a powerful carry tailwind for the pair, which is trading near multi-decade highs as Tokyo keeps its intervention powder dry
AUD.AUD/CAD: AUD/CAD is influenced by extreme CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k and a 60bp carry advantage for the Australian dollar. While Canada's April GDP was strong, the RBA's hawkish minutes and the technical strength in AUD/USD are driving the cross higher as the BoC remains on hold for July 15
AUD.AUD/CHF: AUD/CHF is rising as the RBA's hawkish minutes contrast with the SNB's on-hold stance until September. The pair is supported by a positive risk environment (VIX 16.5) and an improving Australian outlook, even as the Swiss KOF Barometer shows domestic economic recovery
AUD.AUD/NZD: AUD/NZD remains under pressure as the RBNZ is the only G10 central bank with a hike priced in for July 8. Despite hawkish RBA minutes, the kiwi's +335bp OIS advantage over JPY and its leading G10 performance make it the preferred antipodean play in the current regime

CAD.USD/CAD: USD/CAD is testing the 1.42 level as a 0.5% April GDP beat for Canada meets soft May guidance and extreme retail sentiment (94% short). The pair's upside is being checked by crude oil prices moving back below the 100-hour moving average, while CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k suggest institutional bearishness remains entrenched ahead of the July 15 BoC meeting
CAD.EUR/CAD: EUR/CAD is caught between extreme positioning in both currencies, with EUR LF net shorts at -67.5k and CAD LF net shorts at -87.1k. The ECB's hawkish bias and discussion of higher reserve requirements are providing a floor for the euro, while the loonie struggles to capitalize on its GDP beat due to soft forward-looking guidance
CAD.GBP/CAD: GBP/CAD is trading on the divergence between the BoE's steady hand and the BoC's on-hold stance. CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k remain a dominant factor, while the pound finds support from domestic stability and the lack of dovish BoE commentary following Governor Bailey's recent remarks
CAD.AUD/CAD: AUD/CAD is trending higher as hawkish RBA minutes and a technical break in AUD/USD outweigh Canada's recent GDP surprise. CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k and the BoC's on-hold status for July 15 leave the loonie vulnerable to the Australian dollar's superior momentum and carry profile
CAD.CAD/JPY: CAD/JPY is bid as JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k and the BoJ's on-hold stance offset CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k. The yen's fall to 1986 lows against the dollar is providing a lift to the cross, even as the loonie faces its own institutional headwinds and soft May economic guidance
CAD.CAD/CHF: CAD/CHF is consolidating as the SNB remains on hold and CAD COT LF net shorts reach -87.1k. While the Swiss KOF Barometer indicates an improving outlook, the loonie is finding some support from its April GDP beat, though the BoC's on-hold stance for July 15 limits significant upside
CAD.NZD/CAD: NZD/CAD is supported by a hike priced in for the RBNZ on July 8, contrasting with the BoC's on-hold status. CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k and the kiwi's leading G10 performance are driving the pair higher, as the loonie's GDP-driven recovery is hampered by soft May guidance

CHF.USD/CHF: USD/CHF is entering a corrective move as technicals converge and crude oil prices slip below the 100-hour moving average. While the Fed remains on hold, the Swiss KOF Barometer has climbed above its long-term average, suggesting an improving economic outlook that is providing a floor for the franc ahead of the September SNB meeting
CHF.EUR/CHF: EUR/CHF is pressured by EUR LF net short positioning of -67.5k and cooling inflation in major euro zone economies. The SNB remains on hold, but the improving Swiss economic outlook and the ECB's potential shift toward a less urgent hiking cycle are keeping the cross in a defensive posture
CHF.GBP/CHF: GBP/CHF is steady as both the BoE and SNB maintain on-hold stances for their upcoming meetings. The pair is largely driven by broader risk sentiment (VIX 16.5) and the lack of major UK catalysts, while the Swiss KOF Barometer indicates a gradual recovery in domestic activity
CHF.AUD/CHF: AUD/CHF is finding support from hawkish RBA minutes and a technical breakout in the Australian dollar. While the SNB is on hold until September, the AUD's 60bp carry advantage and high volatility premium are attracting buyers in a normal volatility regime (VIX 16.5)
CHF.CAD/CHF: CAD/CHF is range-bound as CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k offset the loonie's recent GDP beat. The SNB's on-hold status and the improving Swiss KOF Barometer are providing a neutral backdrop for the pair as traders wait for the July 15 BoC decision
CHF.CHF/JPY: CHF/JPY is trending higher as the yen hits 39-year lows and JPY COT LF net shorts reach -115.0k. With the BoJ on hold and the SNB also steady, the pair is functioning as a carry play, benefiting from the yen's status as the primary G10 laggard and the Swiss economy's improving fundamentals
CHF.NZD/CHF: NZD/CHF is bid as the RBNZ prepares for a potential hike on July 8, while the SNB remains on hold. The kiwi's leading G10 performance and significant OIS advantage are driving the pair higher, supported by a stable risk environment and the Swiss KOF Barometer's positive trend

NZD.NZD/USD: NZD/USD is facing resistance at 0.5700 despite the RBNZ having a hike priced in for July 8. The pair's recovery is being aided by crude oil prices moving below the 100-hour moving average, which has softened the USD, but the kiwi remains the G10 leader due to its +335bp OIS carry advantage over the yen and hawkish central bank bias
NZD.EUR/NZD: EUR/NZD is trending lower as EUR LF net shorts of -67.5k and cooling German inflation contrast with the RBNZ's hike bias. The New Zealand dollar is outperforming the euro as markets price in a 62% probability of a rate increase at the July 8 meeting, while the ECB remains on hold
NZD.GBP/NZD: GBP/NZD is under pressure as the RBNZ's hawkish stance makes the kiwi the strongest G10 performer. With the BoE on hold and Governor Bailey taking a patient approach to energy prices, the pound is losing ground to the NZD's superior carry and imminent hike expectations
NZD.AUD/NZD: AUD/NZD is trading with a downward bias as the RBNZ's July 8 hike expectation outweighs the RBA's hawkish minutes. While both antipodean currencies are strong, the NZD's clear policy path and leading performance in the G10 space are keeping the cross capped
NZD.NZD/JPY: NZD/JPY is a standout carry trade, supported by a +335bp OIS advantage and JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k. The RBNZ's hike priced for July 8 and the BoJ's on-hold status have pushed the pair higher as the yen hits its weakest level since 1986
NZD.NZD/CAD: NZD/CAD is rising as the RBNZ's hike bias meets CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k. Although Canada's April GDP was firm, the loonie's soft May guidance and the BoC's on-hold stance for July 15 are allowing the kiwi to dominate the cross
NZD.NZD/CHF: NZD/CHF is bid as the RBNZ's July 8 hike expectation contrasts with the SNB's on-hold position. The kiwi is benefiting from its position as the G10 performance leader, while the Swiss franc remains steady amid an improving domestic economic outlook and normal market volatility

USD.EUR/USD: EUR/USD is seeing its downside protected as crude oil prices slip below the 100-hour moving average, though Fed rate-hike bets continue to support the greenback. ECB President Lagarde's hawkish bias and discussions on reserve requirements are being balanced against cooling German inflation, which has eased the immediate pressure for further ECB action
USD.GBP/USD: GBP/USD is steady as the BoE remains on hold and Governor Bailey signals a wait-and-see approach on energy prices. The pair is finding some support from the intraday reversal in crude oil, which has moved below its 100-hour moving average, tempering the dollar's recent momentum
USD.USD/JPY: USD/JPY has surged to 162.61, a 39-year high, as the 275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage remains intact despite a moderate put skew for intervention hedging. JPY COT LF net shorts of -115.0k and the BoJ's on-hold stance for July 31 are driving the pair, even as the correlation with US 10Y yields breaks
USD.AUD/USD: AUD/USD is testing the 100-hour MA at 0.6913, bolstered by hawkish RBA minutes and firmer US confidence data. The pair is also benefiting from crude oil prices falling below their 100-hour moving average, while a massive 10.6% volatility premium suggests the options market is bracing for significant tail risk
USD.USD/CAD: USD/CAD is consolidating near 1.4207 as a strong April GDP beat for Canada is offset by soft May guidance and CAD COT LF net shorts of -87.1k. The pair's upside is limited by crude oil prices moving back below the 100-hour moving average, while extreme retail short positioning suggests a potential divergence at current levels
USD.USD/CHF: USD/CHF is starting a corrective move as technicals coil and the dollar loses some steam following the crude oil reversal. The Swiss KOF Barometer's climb above its long-term average indicates an improving economic outlook, providing support for the franc as the Fed and SNB both remain on hold
USD.NZD/USD: NZD/USD is recovering toward 0.5700, supported by the RBNZ's hike bias for the July 8 meeting. The pair is finding additional tailwinds as crude oil buying runs out of steam, while the kiwi's leading G10 performance and significant carry advantage keep it well-bid against the greenback
